reviews.review_msg: He is very passionate with what he teaches, kind and cares for improvement. He was quite funny and always matched his shirts with each lecture. This material in his course is heavy, and it can be hard but it is manageable. He records his lectures on zoom, and talks slow so being sleepy is likely. The textbook is useful for extra information

Tom is honestly a great prof. Very approachable and nice guy, although the lectures can be a bit long, he records lectures which makes life easier. 90% of the grade was from the 3 noncumilative exams, but each exam only covers 3-4 chapters so you could really focus and do your best. He is open to answer all your questions and deserves higher rating

Really enjoy taking PSYC221 with Tom. Lectures are engaging. Also, they are recorded which is helpful as its almost inevitable to space out during a 3 hour lecture. Three exams (each worth 30%) might sound tough, yet they are non-cumulative. This structure actually made the course much manageable, as I can focus on only 3-4 chapters for each exam.
Tom is great! One of the best psyc professors in the department. Has recorded lectures, three exams worth 30% each and assignments worth 10%.

Dr Spalek is passionate and cares about his students, however lectures can be long and fairly monotonous, so be prepared to sit for a long time. Content is mainly from the studies discussed in lectures. 90% of the grade is from 3 midterms, so not great for those who don't thrive in exam settings.

Dr Spalek is passionate about what he teaches and cares about the students doing well. However, lectures are long and fairly monotonous (also only gives 1 breaks for the 3hrs so be prepared to sit). Interesting class but 90% of the grade is exams so not the best for those who don't do the best in exam settings.

Tom is very nice and approachable, his lectures can be monotonous at times but if you actually engage, you'll find the material interesting, theres 3 exams worth 30% each and they focus a lot on the people, their experiments and the results, remaining 10% are little weekly assignments, nothing super hard, he actually cares and encourages questions!

This professor was excellent and funny. He demonstrated passion for his subject and a great understanding of what was being taught. His grading was harsh, but the weight of the exams seemed pretty typical for a class like this. The material was clear but I would suggest reading ahead where possible for a deeper understanding. TAs were rough though.

A nice guy in general, but the material is hard. His lectures made me super sleepy because his voice is relatively monotone. I eventually realized I learned more by just reading the textbook in my own time. He's okay, but lectures were nothing special and the memorization of experiments he expects gets exhausting quick.

Explains things very clearly, and always checks for student understanding throughout the lecture. Very understanding extensions. 3 multiple choice exams that were non-cumulative and only cover three chapter each.

Dr. Spalek was great in PSYC 221, which is why I took this course. The content here is more dry and very detailed, which made it harder to stay focused during the 3 hour, content heavy lectures. The course has two midterms and a final, so make sure you're staying on top of the lectures/readings.

Thomas is a great professor but the content is very heavy. You have to study the textbook as well as the slides because some of the content is just in the textbook and not on the slides. He was very supportive and always encouraged us to ask for help. This is one of the hardest courses I've taken at SFU but he's a decent prof.
Amazing prof, very good at teaching. Made this semester fun to learn. He explains concepts very well and exams are super super fair. Would 100% take again

If you ever get the chance to take PSYC 221 with Tom Spalek, PLEASE DO IT. He's genuinely one of the best profs I've ever had. He's so kind, funny in a subtle way, and he actually cares if you understand the material. The content can be challenging, but if you put in the effort, it becomes really AMAZING.

Spalek is awesome. I find it hard to focus in lectures (for any prof), so I watch the lecture recordings in a faster speed and its great. The material itself is a little tricky unless you spend a lot of time with it, but you can tell he wants the students to succeed. Provides us with study guides and opportunities for bonus marks at end of sem.

While Tom is a nice person,he talks very slow&monotonous.Many examples&you will need to know them.Tests on things from the book not covered in class.Imo needlessly detailed where it does not matter,eg theories that long disproven. Why so much detail for things that we know are false? I think this just sets students up for confusion in the future.

Dr. Spalek is a fantastic professor! His recorded lectures are clear and easy to follow, and I really enjoyed his teaching style. He's also very nice and approachable, making it easy to ask questions when needed. The exams are straightforward, just study the material and review his study guides, and you'll be good to go. Highly recommend his class!

Really enjoyed 221. The content is super interesting, although his lectures can be a little monotonous. Our weekly multiple choice assignments only take a few minutes. He puts them together to create study guides for the exams which are super helpful. Exams are straight forward with really easy written sections. You just have to like the content.

Dr. Spalek's lectures are slow-paced, but he records them, so you can speed them up for studying. His exams are non-cumulative, so it is not hard to catch up even if you've procrastinated. The weekly assignments are also easy and not time-consuming. Overall, the course content can be challenging, but if you watch the lectures, you should be fine

Tom is very approachable. Whenever I was unsure of something he was able to simplify the concepts and explain them using simple language. The only issue I had was that the emphasis was overly focused on some experiments which I felt were not necessary to learn. If you attend lectures and study then you should be good.

This professor uses lots of examples, the class actually wasn't too hard. 3 exams each worth 30%, then 10% in assignments (takes you literally 5 minutes to do). If you read the textbook and go over the lectures you'll be fine, also for the word answers REMEMBER THE EXPERIMENTS that he shows in class and the results of them, and who did them.
loved the prof and the class super interesting material.
Loved this class and Prof Spalek. Difficult material but not too difficult that it isn't able to be understood. 10/10 would take again
Amazing class. Amazing professor. You can tell he cares about the subject he is teaching. Difficult material but show up and pay attention and you will succeed.

I loved this class. Professor Spalek truly cares about his students and is an amazing teacher. The content is so interesting and you can tell he has a passion for it. This class encapsulates very difficult material but if you pay attention it can be quite easy. Amazing professor would take again.

It is clear that either Spalek either doesn't care about teaching or doesn't believe what he teaches about. Gives great tips on memory retention improvement, but wholly fails to incorporate these methods into his course. The irony would be funny if I wasn't paying so much to attend. Likely hired for research skills bc there is no teaching ability.

Although Tom is a good lecturer, I feel like the way he structures his lectures can be quite confusing at times. The course could have been a lot easier if he had just focused on what we actually needed to know. Class average was always at 60%, but at least the course is graded on a curve.

He is one of the best prof that I ever had. PSYC 221 is a hard course, his exams are quite hard. However, he teaches very well, his classes are fun, you can lean a lot from his and honestly if you go though all the lectures and read the text book, you can get a very good mark. I definitely recommended. ;)

the prof was very nice but this class was really boring. had to memorize a lot of stuff for the exams because they were pretty much the only thing we were graded on. i heard a lot of people didn't pass so i feel lucky despite this class bringing my average down lol

Tom is a really kind person. Very accommodating during COVID. 3 midterms worth 30% each, 10% for participation. Hard to memorize the amount of information in both text and lecture especially with no additional components like papers, projects, or discussion. If you're good with memorization, you'll do well. If you struggle with exams, avoid.
